evals.prompt: I want to add MatrixScan AR to my .NET for iOS app. Here's my empty UIViewController: EmptyViewController.cs. I need to scan EAN-13 and QR barcodes, show a rectangle highlight on each tracked barcode, and log each newly-tracked barcode's data to the console.
evals.expected_output: The skill reads integration.md, fetches the latest Scandit NuGet versions, writes complete BarcodeAr integration code into EmptyViewController.cs (new BarcodeAr(context, settings), BarcodeArView.Create(...) with this.View as the parentView, IBarcodeArHighlightProvider implementation that returns Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?> with a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode), SessionUpdated event handler that iterates session.NewlyTrackedBarcodes and logs each barcode's Data via DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync, barcodeArView.Start() in ViewWillAppear and barcodeArView.Stop() in ViewWillDisappear, Dispose on teardown), and shows the setup checklist (NuGet packages, NSCameraUsageDescription in Info.plist, AppDelegate initialize for SDK 8.0+, SupportedOSPlatformVersion 15.0, license key).

assertions.text: Setup checklist is shown and mentions adding the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode and Scandit.DataCapture.Core NuGet packages
Setup checklist mentions adding NSCameraUsageDescription to Info.plist
Setup checklist mentions adding ScanditCaptureCore.Initialize() and ScanditBarcodeCapture.Initialize() in AppDelegate.FinishedLaunching (required for SDK 8.0+)
Setup checklist mentions setting <SupportedOSPlatformVersion>15.0</SupportedOSPlatformVersion> (or higher) in the .csproj
A license key placeholder string is present
DataCaptureContext.ForLicenseKey( is used to create the context
new BarcodeArSettings() is used (not BarcodeArSettings.Create(), which does not exist)
new BarcodeAr(context, settings) is used as the constructor (not BarcodeAr.Create(...) or BarcodeAr.ForDataCaptureContext(...), which do not exist)
Symbology.Ean13Upca is enabled
Symbology.Qr is enabled
No symbologies other than Ean13Upca and Qr are enabled
Swift-style camelCase symbology names (Symbology.ean13UPCA, Symbology.qr) are NOT used
BarcodeArView.Create( is called (not new BarcodeArView(...), which is not a public constructor)
BarcodeArView.Create( receives a UIView (this.View or a UIView outlet) as the parentView argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives the BarcodeAr instance as the barcodeAr argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives the DataCaptureContext as the dataCaptureContext argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives a BarcodeArViewSettings as the settings argument
BarcodeArView.Create( receives null (or a CameraSettings) as the cameraSettings argument
this.View.AddSubview(barcodeArView) is NOT called (BarcodeArView.Create attaches the view automatically)
IBarcodeArHighlightProvider is implemented
HighlightForBarcodeAsync is implemented and returns a Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?> (the async/Task-based provider, not a delegate)
A new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no context argument is passed)
completionHandler / delegate-based highlight provider pattern is NOT used (that is the Swift pattern, not the .NET pattern)
barcodeArView.HighlightProvider is assigned to the provider instance
Either barcodeAr.SessionUpdated += handler or barcodeAr.AddListener(IBarcodeArListener) is wired
OnObservationStarted and OnObservationStopped are NOT declared (the .NET IBarcodeArListener has only one method)
barcodeArView.Start() is called from ViewWillAppear
barcodeArView.Stop() is called from ViewWillDisappear
barcodeArView.OnResume() is NOT called (that method does not exist on the iOS BarcodeArView — it is Android-only)
barcodeArView.OnPause() is NOT called (that method does not exist on the iOS BarcodeArView — it is Android-only)
barcodeArView.Dispose() is called on teardown (NOT barcodeArView.OnDestroy(), which does not exist in the .NET binding)
No call to BarcodeCapture.Create or barcodeCapture.AddListener appears (BarcodeAr is not BarcodeCapture)
No DataCaptureView, BarcodeCaptureOverlay, or BarcodeBatchBasicOverlay is created (BarcodeArView is the only view needed)
No Camera.GetDefaultCamera() or dataCaptureContext.SetFrameSourceAsync(...) call is present (BarcodeArView manages its own camera internally) (semantic)
DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync (or InvokeOnMainThread) is used to dispatch UI work from the session-updated handler
No Android-style RequestPermissions / CameraPermissionActivity is referenced (iOS handles the prompt via NSCameraUsageDescription)

evals.prompt: Add an info annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS view controller. The annotation should show the barcode's data as a single body row. Here is my view controller: IntegratedViewController.cs
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider implementation that returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>, constructs a BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) with a BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent whose Text is barcode.Data, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider while preserving existing integration.

assertions.text: IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider is implemented
AnnotationForBarcodeAsync is implemented and returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
A new BarcodeArInfoAnnotation(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no context)
BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent is used and its Text is set to barcode.Data
The BarcodeArInfoAnnotationBodyComponent is imported from the Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Info sub-namespace
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
Delegate-based / completionHandler annotation provider pattern is NOT used (the .NET annotation provider is async/Task-based, not delegate-based)
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved
ViewWillAppear continues to call barcodeArView.Start() and ViewWillDisappear continues to call barcodeArView.Stop()

evals.prompt: I want to suppress the default beep and vibration in my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS app — silent scanning. Update the BarcodeAr feedback in IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets barcodeAr.Feedback to a new BarcodeArFeedback() (the empty constructor, which produces a silent feedback), preserving the rest of the integration.

assertions.text: barcodeAr.Feedback is reassigned to a new BarcodeArFeedback() (empty constructor)
BarcodeArFeedback is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.Feedback
BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback is NOT called as a method (BarcodeArFeedback.DefaultFeedback() with parentheses) — it is a static property in .NET
Existing integration code (BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, RectangleHighlightProvider) is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS view controller (IntegratedViewController.cs) I want to style the rectangle highlights: give each tracked barcode a custom brush color and show an icon inside the highlight. Update the highlight provider.
evals.expected_output: The skill keeps the IBarcodeArHighlightProvider returning a BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) and sets the highlight's Brush and Icon properties before returning it. The Brush is built with the iOS UIColor-based Brush constructor (the .NET binding's Brush takes platform UIColor values, not a managed Color type), and the Icon is a ScanditIcon. The existing BarcodeArView wiring and lifecycle are preserved.

assertions.text: The returned highlight is a new BarcodeArRectangleHighlight(barcode) constructed with only the Barcode argument
The highlight's Brush property is assigned
The highlight's Icon property is assigned a ScanditIcon
Brush is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Core.UI.Style
HighlightForBarcodeAsync still returns Task<IBarcodeArHighlight?>
The provider is assigned to barcodeArView.HighlightProvider
A managed Scandit.DataCapture.Core.Color type is NOT used to build the Brush (the .NET iOS Brush constructor takes UIColor values)
barcodeArView.Start() in ViewWillAppear and barcodeArView.Stop() in ViewWillDisappear are preserved

evals.prompt: Add a status icon annotation to each tracked barcode in my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS app. It should show a compact icon that expands to show the short text 'In stock' when the user taps the highlight. Update IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that returns a BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode), sets its Text to 'In stock' and optionally its Icon, sets AnnotationTrigger to HighlightTap so it expands on tap, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider.

assertions.text: IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider is implemented and AnnotationForBarcodeAsync returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
A new BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation(barcode) is constructed with only the Barcode argument (no context)
The annotation's Text property is set to the In stock string
BarcodeArStatusIconAnnotation is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the new provider instance
Delegate-based / completionHandler annotation provider pattern is NOT used (the .NET provider is async/Task-based)
Existing BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, SessionUpdated handler, and RectangleHighlightProvider wiring is preserved

evals.prompt: I want a popover with two action buttons ('Details' and 'Remove') to appear when the user taps a tracked barcode's highlight in my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS app, and I need to know which button was tapped. Update IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill adds an IBarcodeArAnnotationProvider that returns a BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, buttons), where buttons is an IList<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton> each built from a ScanditIcon and text. It assigns a Listener implementing IBarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener whose OnPopoverButtonTapped(popover, button, buttonIndex) identifies the tapped button, leaves EntirePopoverTappable false so per-button taps fire, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider.

assertions.text: A new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation(barcode, buttons) is constructed taking the Barcode and an IList<BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton>
Each button is a new BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton(icon, text) constructed from a ScanditIcon and a text string
BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations.Popover
An IBarcodeArPopoverAnnotationListener implementation is provided and assigned to the annotation's Listener property
OnPopoverButtonTapped is implemented with the signature taking the BarcodeArPopoverAnnotation, the BarcodeArPopoverAnnotationButton, and an int buttonIndex
EntirePopoverTappable is left false (or not set to true) so individual button taps are delivered
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the provider instance
Existing BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, and lifecycle wiring is preserved

evals.prompt: Add a responsive annotation in my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS app: when a tracked barcode is close (large on screen) show its full data, and when it is far away show a compact 'Scan closer' hint. Update IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill returns a BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUpAnnotation, farAwayAnnotation) from AnnotationForBarcodeAsync, where closeUpAnnotation and farAwayAnnotation are BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances. It sets the static BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold to control the close-up vs far-away switch point, and assigns the provider to barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider.

assertions.text: A new BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation(barcode, closeUpAnnotation, farAwayAnnotation) is constructed taking the Barcode and two BarcodeArInfoAnnotation arguments
The close-up and far-away arguments are both BarcodeArInfoAnnotation instances
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation.Threshold is set as a static property (not an instance property)
BarcodeArResponsiveAnnotation is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
AnnotationForBarcodeAsync returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
barcodeArView.AnnotationProvider is assigned to the provider instance
Existing BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, and lifecycle wiring is preserved

evals.prompt: In my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS app I want to open a product detail screen when the user taps a barcode's highlight. Wire up the highlight tap in IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill subscribes to barcodeArView.HighlightForBarcodeTapped (a C# event on BarcodeArView, not a UiListener/UIDelegate property), reads args.Barcode.Data from the HighlightForBarcodeTappedEventArgs, and dispatches the navigation onto the main queue. It does NOT add a UiListener property.

assertions.text: The handler subscribes to barcodeArView.HighlightForBarcodeTapped using the += event-subscription syntax
The handler reads the tapped barcode from the event args (args.Barcode)
HighlightForBarcodeTappedEventArgs is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI
A barcodeArView.UiListener property is NOT assigned (the .NET iOS BarcodeArView exposes the HighlightForBarcodeTapped event instead of a UiListener / UIDelegate)
DispatchQueue.MainQueue.DispatchAsync (or InvokeOnMainThread) is used to run UI work from the tap handler
Existing BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, and lifecycle wiring is preserved

evals.prompt: I want the info annotations in my MatrixScan AR .NET iOS app to appear automatically as soon as a barcode is scanned, instead of only when the user taps the highlight. Update the annotation provider in IntegratedViewController.cs.
evals.expected_output: The skill sets the annotation's AnnotationTrigger property to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan so the annotation shows immediately on scan (and can still be toggled by tapping the highlight), rather than the default-for-tap-only HighlightTap. The provider continues to return the annotation from AnnotationForBarcodeAsync.

assertions.text: The annotation's AnnotationTrigger property is set to BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger.HighlightTapAndBarcodeScan
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger is imported from Scandit.DataCapture.Barcode.Ar.UI.Annotations
BarcodeArAnnotationTrigger is used as an enum value (PascalCase member access), not a Swift-style camelCase .highlightTapAndBarcodeScan
AnnotationForBarcodeAsync still returns Task<IBarcodeArAnnotation?>
Existing BarcodeAr, BarcodeArView, and lifecycle wiring is preserved
